untranslatable_reason: This signal requires calculation of bond-level beta coefficients and cross-sectional ranking/sorting across a bond universe. The Betting Against Beta (BAB) factor strategy requires: (1) computing rolling betas for each bond instrument, (2) partitioning bonds into low-beta and high-beta groups via ranked beta sorting, (3) beta-weighted portfolio construction within each group, (4) rescaling portfolios to target beta of 1.0, and (5) executing a zero-cost long-short spread. None of these operations (beta estimation, cross-sectional ranking, portfolio-level beta targeting, zero-cost arbitrage construction) can be expressed using only the allowed price-action indicators (RSI, MACD, moving averages, Bollinger Bands, ATR, etc.). This is a multi-asset factor strategy requiring portfolio-level analytics, not a single-instrument price-based signal.
translation_notes: BAB is a systematic factor investing strategy that operates at the portfolio construction level with custom risk metrics (beta). It requires access to: (1) historical return time-series for beta regression, (2) cross-sectional asset ranking, (3) portfolio optimization and rescaling. The allowed indicator set is limited to single-instrument technical indicators and cannot support factor-based multi-asset allocation rules. Recommend implementing this as a custom portfolio rebalancer module outside the price-action signal engine.
